content: POPS＆アニメ merged the old "POPS" and "ANIME" categories back in maimai PiNK (December 2015). This is the licensed mainstream section: J-pop hits and anime themes released under major labels. Some tracks are covers rather than original recordings. It is the largest category in the game and the most immediate entry point for players who don't spend their free time watching BMS charts or browsing Niconico. New versions add current hits alongside older standards.

The niconico & VOCALOID category is maimai's direct line to Japanese internet music culture. It includes tracks uploaded to Niconico, songs built on Vocaloid, CeVIO AI, and Synthesizer V, and the wider ecosystem of utaite covers and web-pop. This has been part of the game since 2012, pulling in early Vocaloid standards and newer viral hits alike. It sits alongside POPS & ANIME and Touhou Project as a major category, but its specific territory is creator-driven music that blew up online before it ever reached an arcade cabinet.

東方Project (TOUHOU Project) in maimai means fan-made arrangements, not original game soundtracks. The category pulls from the massive doujin music ecosystem surrounding ZUN's franchise, with tracks from well-known circles and arrangers across electronic, rock, orchestral, and hardcore. It showed up early alongside NicoNico and Game & Variety as part of maimai's nod to internet and doujin culture. The charts tend toward high-energy, technical patterns, and the catalog keeps growing with new arrangements each version.

ゲーム＆バラエティ (Game & Variety) covers everything that isn't POPS, anime, Vocaloid, or maimai original. The category traces back to older "GAME" and "JOYPOLIS" classifications and settled into its current form in maimai でらっくす PLUS. The lineup pulls from classic game soundtracks, SEGA-affiliated attractions, and miscellaneous independent releases. It is also notorious for housing some of the most brutal charts in the game. This is where external game music and oddball tracks live, separate from the commissioned originals and the Gekichumai crossover pool.

The maimai category is SEGA's in-house catalog—music written specifically for the game, not licensed from elsewhere. These are the version theme songs, the boss tracks, and the character pieces composed by SEGA's internal sound team and long-term collaborators like Hiro, Yusuke Seo, and Hironobu Yahata. The sound is unmistakably arcade-oriented, tied to each version's visual identity and lore. The catalog spans electronic, pop, rock, and orchestral, and occasionally absorbs tracks that originated in other SEGA projects but ended up feeling at home here.

The オンゲキ＆CHUNITHM category is the shared file cabinet between SEGA's three Performai games. Along with maimai, these form the Gekichumai family, and this is where their music intersects: boss tracks, arcade exclusives, and character songs from units like オンゲキシューターズ, ⊿TRiEDGE, and bitter flavor. Many tracks arrived through collaborative events and cross-game linkage campaigns, where playing one game unlocked content in another. The category keeps accumulating additions as the three games continue to borrow from each other.

宴会場 (UTAGE) is maimai's sandbox for weird charts. Introduced in later versions, it throws out the standard rules in favor of gimmicks: irregular patterns, visual tricks, mirrored inputs between players, and whatever else the charting team dreams up. Difficulty labels here are often unconventional, and some charts demand coordination between two players rather than solo precision. UTAGE exists for the player who has seen everything and wants something that doesn't play by the book.
